manualGitlabOnly.title: Type-a-repo entry isn't supported on GitLab yet
manualGitlabOnly.body: GitLab needs a numeric project id to create a Merge Request, which can't be typed by hand. Pick your project from the scanned list — or sign in with GitHub if you'd rather paste a repo name.

blockers.title: Anything that should stop the upgrade

blockers.policyBad: {{ count }} blocking violation(s) — fix these before continuing.
blockers.breakingGood: No conflicts detected for this Angular target.
blockers.breakingBad: {{ count }} package(s) won't work on the target Angular version yet.
blockers.deprecatedGood: Nothing in your tree is officially deprecated.
blockers.deprecatedNote: {{ count }} package(s) are deprecated — review before merging.

pr.title: Open the pull request

search.placeholder: Enter an npm package name — try rxjs, @angular/material, zone.js
search.hint: Live data from registry.npmjs.org, npm downloads API, and OSV.dev. Press {{ mod }}+{{ k }} for the command palette.

announce.loaded: Loaded {{ pkg }} with {{ advisories }} known security advisories.

search.welcomeHint: Pick one of these to get started:
search.welcomeSub: The app checks peerDependencies["@angular/core"] against a known set of Angular majors, and shows the best stable version for the Angular you target.

compare.title: Compare two packages
compare.subtitle: See which Angular majors each side supports, side by side.
compare.noCommon: No Angular major is supported by both packages simultaneously.

competitors.title: Or try comparing with alternatives to {{ name }}:

prosCons.title: AI-generated Pros & Cons
prosCons.lede: Compare {{ a }} and {{ b }} on bundle size, adoption, maintenance, and stability — grounded in objective data, not marketing copy.
prosCons.ctaBody: Get an AI-generated head-to-head between {{ a }} and {{ b }} backed by Bundlephobia, NPM downloads, GitHub activity, and release history.
prosCons.ctaHint: Free via our Groq proxy. Add your own API key in Settings for unlimited generations.
prosCons.loading: Comparing the data… this usually takes 3–6 seconds.

error.rateLimitHint: Free proxy quota reached. Add your own Gemini API key in Settings for unlimited generations, or try again in a minute.
error.proxyUnavailableLead: Two ways to make this work:
error.proxyOptionSsr: Run the full SSR server with GROQ_API_KEY set:
error.proxyOptionByo: Or paste your own Gemini / OpenAI / DeepSeek key in Settings — those calls go browser-direct and don't need the proxy.

usageGuide.title: Side-by-side Usage Guide
usageGuide.lede: See exactly how to install, set up, and use each package — generated by AI from each package's docs.
usageGuide.ctaBody: Generate a runnable side-by-side guide showing how to install and integrate {{ a }} and {{ b }} in an Angular project.
usageGuide.ctaHint: Cached for 24 hours so revisits are instant — and free.
usageGuide.loading: Reading the docs and writing the code… 5–10 seconds.

error.rateLimitHint: Free proxy quota reached. Add your own Gemini API key in Settings for unlimited generations, or try again in a minute.
error.proxyUnavailableLead: Two ways to make this work:
error.proxyOptionSsr: Run the full SSR server with GROQ_API_KEY set:
error.proxyOptionByo: Or paste your own Gemini / OpenAI / DeepSeek key in Settings — those calls go browser-direct and don't need the proxy.

aiSettings.title: AI provider settings
aiSettings.privacy: Your API keys never leave this browser. They're stored in localStorage and sent directly to the provider — never to our servers.
aiSettings.experimentalTooltip: We haven't tested this provider against a real key yet. The adapter is built defensively and auto-retries on schema errors, but you may see edge cases — please report them via the thumbs-down button.
aiSettings.experimentalFootnote: These providers are marked experimental because we currently only test against Groq and Gemini. The adapters share most of their code with the verified path and include a self-healing retry, so they should work — but if something breaks, please let us know.

groq.title: Groq
groq.body: Routed through our server with rate limits (10/min, 50/day). No key needed.

gemini.title: Google Gemini
gemini.body: 1500 requests/day with your own key. Generous free tier, no credit card.

paid.title: OpenAI · DeepSeek
paid.body: Highest quality with pay-per-request billing. Use when you need consistent results across hundreds of comparisons.

drop.title: Drop .ts / .html source files (optional)
drop.hint: We'll filter breaking changes to only the ones your code actually touches, with file:line citations. Everything stays in your browser.
drop.aria: Drop TypeScript or HTML source files to run a source-aware breaking-change scan

codemod.title: Auto-fix previews
codemod.empty: No codemods registered for this package yet.
codemod.noHits: No changes — this codemod didn't match anything in your uploaded files.

workspace.title: Workspace dependency drift
workspace.drift: {{ n }} dep(s) with version drift across projects
workspace.noDrift: All dependencies are aligned across projects.

history.title: Search history & favorites
history.subtitle: Recent searches are saved locally in this browser.
history.noFavorites: No favorites yet — star a package from the search page.

compareHistory.title: Recent comparisons

summary.title: The short version
summary.body: ng-package-compat is a developer tool. We collect only what's needed to run sign-in and store your workspace state, we don't sell data to anyone, and you can delete everything we have on you at any time from the Danger Zone on the projects page.

collect.title: What we collect
collect.intro: When you use the app, the following data is captured:

identity.label: Identity
identity.body: Your email and provider profile when you sign in with GitHub, GitLab, BitBucket, or Azure. Stored in Supabase Auth.

workspace.label: Workspace state
workspace.body: Favorites, search history, comparisons, notes, snapshots, and policy rules. Stored locally in IndexedDB / localStorage; for signed-in users, also mirrored to Supabase so it syncs across devices.

tokens.label: OAuth tokens
tokens.body: Provider tokens are stored in memory only and never sent to our servers. Personal access tokens you paste are kept locally to enable repository scanning.

errors.label: Error telemetry
errors.body: Uncaught exceptions and the typed AI errors are sent to Sentry to help us diagnose bugs. Tokens, API keys, and personally identifiable form input are scrubbed before transmission.

collect.byo: BYO AI keys you paste (Gemini, OpenAI, DeepSeek) live ONLY in this browser's localStorage. They are sent directly from your browser to that provider — they never touch our servers, our logs, or our error tracker.

thirdParties.title: Third parties that touch your data
thirdParties.intro: We rely on a small set of vendors. Each link below points at their own privacy policy:
thirdParties.supabase: Database + authentication. Stores your sign-in identity and workspace state. Subject to Supabase's privacy policy.
thirdParties.groq: AI inference for the free-tier Pros & Cons, Usage Guide, and Competitor Suggestions features. Receives the prompts you generate; does not receive your account identity.
thirdParties.sentry: Error tracking. Receives stack traces and the URL where an error happened. Subject to Sentry's privacy policy.
thirdParties.publicApis: Public APIs we query without sending your identity — only the package name you searched.

storage.title: Where your data lives
storage.body: Local data sits in IndexedDB and localStorage in this browser. Signed-in users also have a copy in Supabase keyed by user id with row-level security; only you can read your own rows. Signing out wipes the local copy on this device but leaves your cloud copy intact for the next sign-in.

rights.title: Your rights
rights.body: You can permanently delete every byte we have on you — auth identity, favorites, history, comparisons, notes, snapshots, policies — with one click. The button lives in the Danger Zone at the bottom of the projects page:

contact.title: Questions or requests
contact.body: Open an issue on the project's GitHub repository, or email the maintainer listed in the package.json. We respond to deletion and access requests within 7 days.

about.title: About ng-package-compat
about.lede: A small Angular app that answers one question for busy teams: “Does this npm package work with the Angular version I'm on?”
about.allClient: All requests happen client-side, so you only read what you ask for.

upgrade.title: Angular upgrade & dependency health
upgrade.subtitle: Drop your package.json — optionally alongside angular.json, tsconfig.json, .browserslistrc, or your lockfile. We'll run a full compatibility audit, estimate the effort, flag breaking changes, generate install and rollback commands, and export the plan as Markdown or a CI pipeline.
upgrade.orPaste: Or paste a list of packages (one per line)
upgrade.empty: Drop a package.json or paste a dependency list to begin.

cmd.nxMigrateHint: Run from the workspace root. Commits are created per migration.
cmd.oneShotHint: Run this from your project root.
cmd.installHint: For packages without ng update schematics.
cmd.rollbackHint: Run if the upgrade breaks something — restores your original versions.
cmd.verifyHint: Final sanity check: production build + headless tests.
cmd.verifyOk: Install plan looks clean ({{ n }} packages walked, no peer conflicts).
cmd.verifyBad: Install would fail — {{ n }} peer conflict(s):

table.caption: Per-package compatibility report
table.standaloneTitle: Supports standalone APIs at the target

breakingCallout.body: Review conflict rows — these packages have no release that supports Angular {{ ng }} yet, or the current pinned version is incompatible. Consider alternatives or fork/patch plans.

addNote.title: 💬 Contribute a gotcha
addNote.hint: Know a bug that isn't in the official docs? Share it with the next team that hits it. Notes are stored in your browser.
addNote.intro: Know a bug that isn't in the official docs? Share it with the next team that hits it.
addNote.bodyPlaceholder: What broke, and how you fixed it.

hintLabels.needPkg: Add a package name to continue.
hintLabels.needBody: Note needs at least {{ min }} characters — you have {{ have }}.

toast.needBody: Note must be at least {{ min }} characters (you have {{ have }})

relocation.title: 📦 Package has moved
relocation.autoRedirect: We redirected your search to the new location.

deadButWorking.title: ☠️ Abandoned but usable
deadButWorking.hint: This package is no longer maintained, but an older version still works on your Angular major.
deadButWorking.lastWorking: Last working version for Angular {{ ng }}
deadButWorking.caveat: No security fixes will arrive — plan to migrate to the alternative below.

shortcuts.title: Keyboard shortcuts
shortcuts.tip: Press ? anywhere to reopen this sheet.

preferences.title: Appearance & accessibility
preferences.densityWideHint: Use most of the screen — IDE feel

welcome.title: Welcome to ng-package-compat
welcome.body: Check any npm package against any Angular version, plan upgrades, and monitor dependency health — all in a few seconds.

search.title: Search any package
search.body: Start typing a package name. Autocomplete shows weekly downloads, latest version, and Angular support at a glance.

analyze.title: Drop a package.json
analyze.body: The Upgrade Assistant reads your real dependencies, flags breaking changes, and generates a single copyable ng update command.

upgrade.title: One panel, every signal
upgrade.body: Compatibility, bundle size, peer conflicts, security advisories, license risk, release freshness — all in one report.

preferences.title: Tune it to your eyes
preferences.body: Switch accent colors, resize text, enable high contrast, or pick a color-blind-safe palette from the cog in the corner.

shortcuts.title: Keyboard first
shortcuts.body: Press ? any time to see every shortcut. Ctrl/Cmd + K opens the command palette for quick jumps and actions.

notes.title: Notes
notes.flag: 📌 Pin this package to the top
notes.placeholder: Why is this package special? e.g. pinned for legal review.

favorites.title: Favorites
favorites.subtitle: Live health dashboard for the packages you star. Click any row to run a fresh analysis.
favorites.emptyBody: Star packages from the search page to see them here with live version, downloads, and health.

providerExperimental.label: Experimental
providerExperimental.tooltip: {{ name }} integration is experimental — we ship the sign-in flow and the adapter, but we haven't been able to test against a real account yet. Sign-in should work; repo enumeration may need org-level configuration. If something breaks, please report it.

signIn.title: Sign in to scan your Angular projects
signIn.lede: Connect a code host once — we'll discover every Angular project you can read and run a compatibility check on each one.
signIn.directLede: One-click sign-in. We immediately list your repos and analyze every Angular project we find.

errors.unknown: Something went wrong on the way back from the sign-in provider. Please try again.
errors.access_denied: You declined to share access with ng-package-compat. If that wasn’t intentional, try again and approve the requested permissions.
errors.invalid_scope: The sign-in provider rejected one of the requested permissions. Try again with a different provider, or contact support if this keeps happening.
errors.invalid_request: The sign-in request was malformed. Try again from the sign-in page.
errors.unauthorized_client: This app isn’t authorized to use the selected sign-in provider. Try a different provider.
errors.unsupported_response_type: The sign-in provider returned an unsupported response. Try again or pick a different provider.
errors.server_error: The sign-in provider had a server error. It’s usually temporary — please try again in a moment.
errors.temporarily_unavailable: The sign-in provider is temporarily unavailable. Please try again shortly.
errors.interaction_required: The sign-in provider needs further interaction. Try again and complete every step.
errors.login_required: The sign-in provider needs you to log in again. Please try once more.
errors.consent_required: You’ll need to approve permissions on the sign-in screen. Try again and accept when asked.
errors.session_timeout: Sign-in took too long to complete. This usually means the OAuth round-trip didn’t return cleanly — please try again.

projects.heading: Your Angular projects
projects.subtitle: Repos discovered through {{ provider }}
projects.title: Repositories
projects.found: {{ angular }} Angular projects out of {{ total }} repos

empty.title: No projects yet
empty.body: We didn't find any repositories on this account. Try the LinkedIn workspace if you want to connect a different host.
empty.azureHint: Azure DevOps integration is experimental — if you signed in with Microsoft and don't see repos, it may be because the adapter hasn't been tested against all org configurations. Try GitHub, GitLab, or BitBucket as alternatives, or let us know what you're seeing.

noToken.title: We've lost your provider token
noToken.body: Provider tokens don't survive page reloads. Sign in again to refresh it.

bitbucket.title: Bitbucket workspaces
bitbucket.body: Bitbucket Cloud now requires a workspace slug to list repos (Atlassian CHANGE-2770). Add yours below — find it in the URL of any repo: bitbucket.org/{{ '{' }}workspace{{ '}' }}/repo.
bitbucket.hint: Lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens. Example: aj769-workspace.

connections.title: Connected code hosts
connections.lede: Link any combination of GitHub, GitLab, BitBucket, and Azure DevOps. We'll merge their projects into one list.
connections.needPat: no token — paste a PAT below

empty.title: Connect a code host to get started
empty.body: Link GitHub, GitLab, BitBucket, or Azure to see your Angular projects here.

deleteAccount.sectionLede: These actions are permanent and cannot be undone.
deleteAccount.title: Delete your account
deleteAccount.warning: This will permanently delete the account associated with {{ email }} and everything we store for you on our servers.
deleteAccount.willLose1: All linked code hosts (GitHub, GitLab, BitBucket, Azure) will be disconnected.
deleteAccount.willLose2: Favorites, search history, comparisons, notes, and snapshots will be erased everywhere — on this device and on our servers.
deleteAccount.willLose3: Your sign-in identity is removed. You won't be able to recover this account; you'd have to sign up again.

versionMigration.modeBanner: Migration mode — analyzing version-to-version changes for {{ pkg }}.
versionMigration.title: Version migration analysis
versionMigration.lede: AI-generated upgrade guide for {{ pkg }} {{ from }} → {{ to }}.
versionMigration.ctaBody: Generate a migration plan for {{ pkg }} from {{ from }} to {{ to }}.
versionMigration.pickVersions: Pick the version you have today and the version you want to upgrade to.
versionMigration.pickDifferent: Pick two different versions to see what changed.
versionMigration.emptyAllClear: No breaking changes, deprecations, or migration steps reported for this range. Drop-in safe.

desc.patch: Bug fixes only. No API changes — safe to upgrade.
desc.minor: Additive only. New APIs, no breaking changes.
desc.major-safe: Major version bump with no breaking changes that affect typical usage.
desc.major-breaking: Contains breaking changes. Read the migration steps before upgrading.

confidence.lowHint: No changelog source was found — output is based on general knowledge and may be incomplete.

deprecated.heading: This package has been deprecated
deprecated.bodyGeneric: The maintainer has marked the latest published version as deprecated. Consider switching to an alternative before adopting.

funding.ariaLabel: Sponsor this package on its funding page (opens in a new tab)

desc.safe: Permissive license — free for commercial and proprietary use, only requires preserving the notice.
desc.weak: Weak copyleft — modifications to this library must be open-sourced, but linking from a closed-source product is fine.
desc.strong: Strong copyleft — distributing software that incorporates this may require open-sourcing the whole work. Review with legal.
desc.proprietary: Proprietary or commercial-only license. Read the LICENSE file before shipping.
desc.unknown: License not recognized or not declared. Open the repository's LICENSE file to verify.

provenance.verifiedLong: This release was published with cryptographic provenance via Sigstore — we can verify which CI workflow built and signed it.

scorecard.label: Scorecard
scorecard.ariaLabel: OpenSSF Scorecard score: {{ score }} out of 10

installScripts.heading: This package runs an install script
installScripts.body: The package declares {{ hooks }} — these scripts execute on your machine during `npm install`. Common for native bindings, but also the primary supply-chain attack vector. Review the package's source before installing in production.

typosquat.heading: Did you mean a different package?
typosquat.body: You searched for "{{ searched }}", which is very close to the popular package "{{ suggestion }}". Typosquats often impersonate popular names to deliver malicious code.

desc.active: Pushed within the last 90 days — actively maintained.
desc.maintained: Pushed within the last year — receiving updates, just not weekly.
desc.slow: Pushed within the last two years — minor updates, may not respond quickly to issues.
desc.inactive: No pushes in over two years — likely unmaintained.
desc.archived: Archived by the maintainer — read-only and will not receive any further updates.
